pr.title: Match more functions and data in `ftCo_Damage`
pr.author: mchen91

summary: Matched additional code and data in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_Damage.c`. The PR body named `ftCo_DamageFly_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys`, `ftCo_8008E5A4`, and `ftCo_8008E9D0`; the decomp.dev bot reported 6 new matches, including `.sdata2`, for +1892 matched code bytes and +64 matched data bytes. The diff is mainly codegen-oriented restructuring: local ordering, stack padding, `GET_FIGHTER` use, helper extraction, `dont_inline`, and `.sdata2` symbol renames.

key_files.role: Reworked ftCommon damage functions, locals, helpers, stack padding, and call shapes to complete several matches.
Renamed several `.sdata2` constants from address labels to compiler-style `@NNN` symbols, contributing to matched data.

what_changed: The bot report also credited a full `.sdata2` match and improvements to still-unmatched `ftCo_8008EC90` and `ftCo_8008EB58`.

`ftCo_8008E5A4` was reshaped around knockback/stick vector math: direct `fp->input.lstick` reads, combined `Vec3` declarations, earlier `f3`/`f30` computation, `PSVECCrossProduct` sign handling, and a separate `scale` variable before applying `cosf`/`sinf`.
`inlineF0` now obtains the fighter with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` and removes an extra scope around `motion_id` checks.

`ftCo_8008E9D0` now computes `scaled_kb = ftCo_ScaleBy154(fp->dmg.kb_applied)` and calls `ftCo_8008DA4C(gobj, fp->dmg.x1860_element, ftCo_8008D8E8(scaled_kb))` instead of the previous argument pattern.
`ftCo_8008EC90` was adjusted to call `ftCo_8008E9D0(gobj)` directly in one branch instead of gating it through `inlineB0(gobj)`.

Several functions switched from `gobj->user_data` to `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)`, including `ftCo_8008F744`, `inlineC0`, `ftCo_Damage_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Anim`, `doFlyRoll`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys`, `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim`, and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys`.
`ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold` was wrapped in `#pragma push`, `#pragma dont_inline on`, and `#pragma pop`.
`inlineD0` was restructured to guard on `fp->mv.co.damage.x8 != 0`, decrement internally, spawn effect `1032` with `&trajectory`, and reset the threshold only when the countdown reaches zero.
`ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim` now uses `PAD_STACK(8)` and calls `inlineD0(gobj)` unconditionally, relying on the guard inside `inlineD0`.
A new shared helper `inlineE0` contains the air-physics choice between `ft_80084DB0` and `ft_80084EEC` based on `fp->x221C_b6`; both `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys` and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys` call it.

`config/GALE01/symbols.txt` changed `.sdata2` names such as `lbl_804D84D0`, `lbl_804D84E0`, `lbl_804D84E8`, `lbl_804D84EC`, `lbl_804D84F0`, `lbl_804D84F8`, and `lbl_804DA718` to `@216`, `@220`, `@320`, `@321`, `@460`, `@521`, and `@637`.
smart_moves: Moved the `mv.co.damage.x8` nonzero check into `inlineD0`, allowing callers like `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim` to call the helper unconditionally while preserving behavior and matching code shape.
Factored the repeated `x221C_b6` airborne physics branch into `inlineE0`, reducing duplicated branch structure in `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys` and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Phys`.
Used `#pragma dont_inline` around `ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold`, a targeted way to preserve a call boundary when the compiler would otherwise alter layout.
Used `PAD_STACK(8)` and a local dummy stack array only where needed, while removing other dummy arrays that no longer matched the desired stack frame.
Converted generic `.sdata2` address labels to compiler-style `@NNN` names, matching how local constants are represented in the symbols file.
Preserved exact floating-point operation ordering in `ftCo_8008E5A4` by reshaping temporaries rather than changing the underlying knockback angle logic.
decomp_lessons: For close fighter-action matches, small changes to local declaration order, temporary lifetimes, and block structure can matter as much as control-flow changes.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` versus direct `gobj->user_data` access can affect generated code and should be tried when matching ftCommon functions.
Centralizing a repeated branch in a helper can be a matching tactic, not just a readability refactor, when two callbacks need the same call/branch shape.
Use `#pragma dont_inline` selectively when a small helper must remain a separate function for matching.
Move guards into inline/helper functions when the assembly suggests callers invoke the helper unconditionally.
For `.sdata2` progress, audit symbol names as well as C code; replacing `lbl_804D...` labels with `@NNN` compiler-style constants can unlock data matches.
Stack-shaping should be minimal and localized: this PR both removed obsolete dummy arrays and added `PAD_STACK(8)` or `u8 _[12]` where the new match required them.
naming_conventions: Compiler-generated `.sdata2` constants may be named with `@NNN`, such as `@216`, `@220`, `@320`, `@321`, `@460`, `@521`, and `@637`, instead of address labels like `lbl_804D84D0`.
Unidentified ftCommon functions kept the project convention of `ftCo_` plus address-like suffixes, such as `ftCo_8008E5A4`, `ftCo_8008E9D0`, and `ftCo_8008EC90`.
Named action callbacks follow the `ftCo_Damage*_{Anim,IASA,Phys}` style, shown by `ftCo_DamageFly_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageFly_Phys`, and `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim`.
Small local helpers in this area still used placeholder names like `inlineD0`, `inlineE0`, and `inlineF0` when no semantic name was established.
`GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` is the preferred-looking accessor pattern in the matched ftCommon snippets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replace direct `gobj->user_data` reads with `GET_FIGHTER(gobj)` when the target assembly suggests a different access pattern.
Use `PAD_STACK(8)` or explicit dummy stack arrays only in functions where the stack frame needs shaping, as seen in `ftCo_DamageFlyRoll_Anim` and `inlineD0`.
Wrap small helpers with `#pragma dont_inline on` to force a separate call boundary, as done for `ftCo_Damage_SetMv8FromKbThreshold`.
Tune float temporary order and reuse carefully: `ftCo_8008E5A4` reuses `kb_mag`, introduces `scale`, and changes when input fields are loaded.
Pass the actual local address directly to calls when needed for stack/codegen shape, as `inlineD0` changed from a copied `param` to `&trajectory` in `efAsync_Spawn`.
Factor repeated branches into a static helper to alter call structure, as with `inlineE0` for `ft_80084DB0` versus `ft_80084EEC`.
Update `.sdata2` entries in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt` to the compiler-generated `@NNN` labels when decomp.dev reports data-match gaps.

review_feedback.feedback: Reported matched code 55.55% (+0.05%, +1892 bytes) and matched data 34.71% (+0.01%, +64 bytes).
Reported improvements in unmatched items `ftCo_8008EC90` (+61 bytes) and `ftCo_8008EB58` (+11 bytes).
No human review comments or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

follow_up_queries: DamageFly_Phys DamageFlyRoll_Phys inlineE0 x221C_b6 ft_80084DB0 ft_80084EEC
